column2: FsF-F1-01MD - Metadata and data are assigned a globally unique identifier.
column3: FsF-F1-02MD - Metadata and data are assigned a persistent identifier.
column4: FsF-F2-01M - Metadata includes descriptive core elements (creator, title, data identifier, publisher, publication date, summary and keywords) to support data findability.
column5: FsF-F3-01M - Metadata includes the identifier of the data it describes.
column6: FsF-F4-01M - Metadata is offered in such a way that it can be registered or indexed by search engines.
column7: FsF-A1-01M - Metadata contains access level and access conditions of the data.
column8: FsF-A1.1-01MD - A standardized communication protocol is used to access metadata and data.
column9: FsF-A1.2-01MD - Metadata and data are accessible through a standardized communication protocol which supports authentication.
column10: FsF-A1-02MD - Metadata and data are retrievable by their identifier
column11: FsF-I1-01M - Metadata is represented using a formal knowledge representation language.
column12: FsF-I2-01M - Metadata uses registered semantic resources
column13: FsF-I3-01M - Metadata includes qualified references between the data and its related entities.
column14: FsF-R1-01M - Metadata specifies the content of the data.
column15: FsF-R1.1-01M - Metadata includes license information under which data can be reused.
column16: FsF-R1.2-01M - Metadata includes provenance information about data creation or generation.
